prompts.label: When to choose Rust
prompts.text:
Objective list when Rust is worth learning vs staying on Go/Python.

- Systems, WASM, performance critical
- Steeper learning curve honest note

5 bullets.

prompts.label: Option chains map and_then
prompts.text:
Show rewriting nested match on Option using map and then and_then.
Compare readability trade-offs for newcomers.
Mention analogous Result combinators.
Discuss when explicit match is still clearer.
Give a toy parsing example.

prompts.label: derive(Debug, Clone) ergonomics
prompts.text:
Explain deriving Debug for printing structs during development.
Explain Clone versus Copy distinctions.
Mention customizing Debug formatting alternatives lightly.
Warn about derives increasing compile times on huge structs mildly.
Point to serde Serialize as a later adjacent topic.

prompts.label: Cargo features toggling optional crates
prompts.text:
Explain declaring optional dependencies and enabling them via features.
Show cfg(feature = "...") guarded code paths.
Warn about unintended feature unification surprises.
Advise documenting feature meanings in Cargo.toml metadata.
Mention shrinking dependency graphs for binaries.

prompts.label: Mutex guard scopes shrink
prompts.text:
Demonstrate shortening critical sections using explicit scopes.
Discuss deadlock risks from nesting locks accidentally.
Mention RwLock read-heavy workloads lightly.
Relate Tokio Mutex for async contexts as a branching note.
Encourage fearless concurrency but not careless locking.

prompts.label: dyn Trait object trade-offs
prompts.text:
Compare trait objects versus monomorphized generics for plugin-like designs.
Mention Sized and object safety constraints at a headline level.
Discuss vtable overhead qualitatively without benchmarks religion.
Advise measuring hot paths.
Give a pragmatic "registry of handlers" intuition.

prompts.label: thiserror versus anyhow boundaries
prompts.text:
Explain using thiserror in libraries exporting structured errors.
Explain using anyhow in applications often needs context.
Mention chaining sources for debugging readability.
Advise mapping errors across boundaries cleanly.
Mention integrating with tracing casually.

prompts.label: Lifetime elision in functions
prompts.text:
Teach common elision rules returning references tied to inputs.
Show examples where Rust forces explicit annotations.
Encourage reading compiler errors literally and slowly.
Suggest preferring owned data in small programs to reduce fights.
Mention the static lifetime for string literals as intuition training.

prompts.label: Iterator adapters laziness
prompts.text:
Explain iterators are lazy until you consume them with collect/for_each.
Show map/filter/take chaining on a synthetic example.
Compare readability vs handwritten loops honesty.
Mention fused adapters only as trivia.
Point to Clippy suggesting idioms.

prompts.label: rustfmt plus clippy in CI
prompts.text:
Encourage cargo fmt --check in CI alongside clippy denying warnings selectively.
Explain allow directives should be justified in comments culturally.
Mention pinning toolchains with rust-toolchain.toml optionally.
Advise newcomers to normalize editor format-on-save flows.
Connect to reproducible CI images.

prompts.label: Integration tests folder layout
prompts.text:
Explain integration tests importing the crate externally from tests/ folder.
Mention helpers module patterns without creating dependency cycles.
Advise tagging slow suites separately loosely.
Mention benchmarks with criterion optionally as reading.
Encourage deterministic tests.
